items.headline: Playoff Injury Bug Bites Hard: Williams, Fox, Harper Sidelined!
items.summary: The Western Conference Finals are grappling with significant injury concerns. OKC's Jalen Williams exited Game 2 with a recurring left hamstring issue, marking his 36th game missed this season due to hamstring problems. Meanwhile, the Spurs were without All-Star De'Aaron Fox (ankle), and his replacement, Dylan Harper, also went down with a leg injury during Game 2.

items.headline: Timberwolves Linked to Kyrie Irving Trade Speculation
items.summary: Rumors are heating up around the league as the Minnesota Timberwolves have emerged as a potential suitor for Kyrie Irving this offseason. Reports suggest a possible trade package could be centered around Julius Randle and draft picks.

items.headline: Israeli Basketball Player Raz Adam Dies at 26
items.summary: Tragic news as Israeli basketball player Raz Adam, 26, passed away in a fatal car crash on Wednesday. Adam was a former youth teammate of NBA star Deni Avdija.

items.headline: Thunder Even Series with Game 2 Win!
items.summary: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's 30 points propelled the Thunder to a crucial Game 2 victory, tying the Western Conference Finals series at 1-1.

items.headline: Knicks Host Cavs in Pivotal Game 2 Tonight
items.summary: After a stunning comeback in Game 1, the New York Knicks look to take a 2-0 lead against the Cleveland Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference Finals. Cavaliers will try to bounce back and avoid an 0-2 deficit before heading home.

items.headline: SGA's Milestone Night
items.statContext: In Game 2 vs. SAS, his 27th career 30-point playoff game, passing Russell Westbrook for 2nd in Thunder history.

items.headline: Wemby's Dominant Stat Line
items.statContext: In Game 2 loss to OKC. Has 62 points and 41 rebounds through two WCF games, a feat not seen since Kareem Abdul-Jabbar in 1974.

items.headline: Brunson Leads Historic Comeback
items.statContext: Fueled Knicks' 22-point 4th quarter comeback for an OT win over Cavs in ECF Game 1.

items.headline: Knueppel Earns All-Rookie First Team Nod
items.statContext: Named to NBA All-Rookie First Team after leading the league in 3-pointers made (273) and recording 36 games with 20+ points.

items.headline: Conference Finals Update: East & West Series Heating Up
items.summary: The NBA Playoffs are in full swing! The New York Knicks hold a 1-0 lead over the Cleveland Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference Finals, while the Oklahoma City Thunder and San Antonio Spurs are tied at 1-1 in a hard-fought Western Conference Finals series.

items.headline: Offseason Buzz: Giannis, LeBron, Harden in Trade Talks?
items.summary: Beyond the Kyrie Irving rumors, the NBA offseason is already generating significant buzz. Speculation continues around Giannis Antetokounmpo and potential landing spots for LeBron James, while the Cavaliers are expected to retain James Harden.

items.headline: Season-Ending Injuries Impacting Several Teams
items.summary: A number of key players are out for the season with significant injuries. Moses Moody (GSW) is out with a torn patellar tendon, Jimmy Butler (MIA) with a torn right ACL, Fred VanVleet (HOU) with a torn ACL, and Damian Lillard (POR) with an Achilles injury.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Breakout Season for Portland
items.summary: Deni Avdija has had an All-Star caliber breakout season for the Portland Trail Blazers, solidifying his role as a foundational piece for the franchise's future. His growth and versatility as a point forward have impressed scouts across the league.
